title: Compute safe slice length as mul(gt(end, start), sub(end, start)) — zero when start >= end
description: When computing a slice length from start and end indices, sub(end, start) would underflow if start > end. mul(gt(end, start), sub(end, start)) returns 0 when start >= end (since gt returns 0, mul by 0 = 0) and the correct length when end > start. This avoids a branch while preventing underflow.
pattern_after: let len := mul(gt(end, start), sub(end, start))
when_to_apply: Any length computation from two indices where the result should be 0 rather than revert when bounds are inverted
when_not_to_apply: When inverted bounds should cause a revert rather than silently returning zero
